content:
> We already share common code for the two different app templates; why is doing so with two plugin templates more confusing?

I had completely overlooked this.

I've added the shared files in `plugin_shared` now.

> Did you mean the website hasn't been changed? Or that comment in the readme. The comment in the readme is applicable.

I mean that you marked the following comment on that line as resolved (which that new comment of mine was a reply to, but maybe that context was lost somehow):
> s/under the same directory/in this directory/
>
> (Saying "the same" directory is confusing, since usually the first run is "flutter create ... foo" in the directory above this one.)

But you didn't make the change I asked for (or reply to explain why you didn't).

> But you didn't make the change I asked for (or reply to explain why you didn't).

My bad, not sure how that happened. I made the change now.
@stuartmorgan @blasten I'd like to go ahead and merge this so it's in the branch cut. Any more comments?

> @stuartmorgan @blasten I'd like to go ahead and merge this so it's in the branch cut. Any more comments?

You posted this question on a holiday for us, and landed it before we started work. I haven't had any work days in which I could have reviewed this since your last update.

Were the NDK issues ever actually fully discussed and reviewed? There was discussion of having a design doc for that, but I never saw one. That was a major blocking point for this work, so if not this should absolutely be reverted until it has been.

> You posted this question on a holiday for us, and landed it before we started work. I haven't had any work days in which I could have reviewed this since your last update.

Oops, my bad! Apologies.

I had misremembered https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/94101#discussion_r768010184 as the NDK review.

Thanks for reverting @zanderso. I'll reupload the PR.
@dcharkes For some more context on the revert, this PR likely landed too late to make the branch cut, anyway, so there's plenty of time to give everyone a chance to review and lgtm.

@dcharkes for an abundance of clarify: please always wait for (a) an LGTM from either the owner of the code (e.g. for flutter_tools, that would be @christopherfujino) or someone that that owner has delegated to, _and_ (b) an LGTM from anyone who has left comments previously, before landing anything. If you need to land something in a hurry please escalate to me before landing. Thanks!

## [webview_flutter] not working choose file (input type file)

<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.io/

     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first:  https://flutter.io/bug-reports/
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!--
     Please tell us exactly how to reproduce the problem you are running into.

     Please attach a small application (ideally just one main.dart file) that
     reproduces the problem. You could use https://gist.github.com/ for this.

     If the problem is with your application's rendering, then please attach
     a screenshot and explain what the problem is.
-->

1. Run simple index.html with tag `<input type="file">` in webview_flutter
2. Try to click to input (choose file) and nothing happens

I have this issue too.
Does anybody have found a solution yet?
I having similiar issues too. I can not select the add media button in my webview and also the choose select button also not function.. other thing are fine..😢
I have this issue too on Android. but run on iOS fine.
The input option does not work with the plugin for me too. :cry:

Hey, I have a workaround for this. This will take another plugin though. :smile: 
The plugin: https://github.com/fluttercommunity/flutter_webview_plugin
The code:
``` body: Center(
        child: WebviewScaffold(
          url: _url,
          withLocalStorage: true,
        ),
      ),
```

this is an issue with native android webviews.  it is not directly related to flutter (a bug within flutter), although fixing it would require the flutter team to write some code to implement the feature as the webview_flutter plugin acts as a passthrough to the native webviews.

you can see a discussion around the android native problem at:
[url](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5907369/file-upload-in-webview)

agreed that this does not affect iOS as the native iOS implementation does not suffer from the same limitation as the android implementation.

i would love to see this get fixed as I spent a ton of time implementing something on a webpage with javascript that you can't do easily in flutter only to have it not work due to the inability to upload files.  i guess i could technically put a flutter widget above the webview to do the upload and then have the webview take over, but that would create a pretty tricky/possibly ugly experience.

> Hey, I have a workaround for this. This will take another plugin though. 😄
> The plugin: https://github.com/fluttercommunity/flutter_webview_plugin
> The code:
> 
> ```
>         child: WebviewScaffold(
>           url: _url,
>           withLocalStorage: true,
>         ),
>       ),
> ```

This plugin doesn't allow you to draw widget on top of webview you need to close the webview to show popup and everything. Also, the "input" tag works fine without "withlocalstorage:true" "withlocalstorage" is used to render HTML/js from the local storage.

> this is an issue with native android webviews. it is not directly related to flutter (a bug within flutter), although fixing it would require the flutter team to write some code to implement the feature as the webview_flutter plugin acts as a passthrough to the native webviews.
> 
> you can see a discussion around the android native problem at:
> [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5907369/file-upload-in-webview](url)
> 
> agreed that this does not affect iOS as the native iOS implementation does not suffer from the same limitation as the android implementation.
> 
> i would love to see this get fixed as I spent a ton of time implementing something on a webpage with javascript that you can't do easily in flutter only to have it not work due to the inability to upload files. i guess i could technically put a flutter widget above the webview to do the upload and then have the webview take over, but that would create a pretty tricky/possibly ugly experience.

Something like this:
```text
webview_flutter:
    git:
      url: git@github.com:fesp/plugins.git
      path: packages/webview_flutter/
      ref: 42fdb08fe0d4bbbf7e0742a05e6343eb789e4b1d
```

I had same problem with NullPointerException on checkPermission method and I resolved it by replacing `Actvity` with `Context`.

Check this commit https://github.com/michaltrmac/plugins/commit/9376b1e15998c2d46ac14ae3d41d913a3bcf0f93 for more info.

This work for my app.

> There is only one problem for me. My html code is:
> `<input type="file" accept="image/*" capture>`
> 
> Now it's possible to select a file. But I need the both option 1-Select a file, or 2-Take a picture.
> The same as Chrome do.
> 
> Does anyone know how to do it?

@brnyza You have to request `CAMERA` permission.
Add 
```xml
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.CAMERA" />
```
to `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` and use some plugin to request permissions in app.

I use this plugin https://pub.dev/packages/permission_handler and then in app request permission with code like this:
```dart
PermissionStatus status = await Permission.camera.status;
if (status.isUndetermined) {
    await Permission.camera.request();
}
```

I also fully uninstalled the app from phone and install it again.
